message:
chore(beads): file 3 review-mode bugs (ft-cbeyl, ft-e8brh, ft-b677n)

Audit findings from commits b94832a7, 6f41130c, 1bde42b3, 6a16ae0a:

- ft-cbeyl [MEDIUM]: mcp_manifest_golden db-gated tool whitelist
  silently drifts when tool renamed or removed — whitelist check is
  !no_db.contains(X) only, passes trivially when X no longer exists
  in either manifest.
- ft-e8brh [MEDIUM]: cancel_races_child_creation test has weak race
  coverage; barrier synchronizes thread start but not cancel timing;
  20 rounds with 8 creators on a multi-core machine typically run all
  children to completion before cancel fires, so the test passes via
  propagation without exercising the interesting interleaving.
- ft-b677n [MEDIUM]: wgpu readback poll loop (wgpu 25 migration) has
  no test coverage for PollType::Poll semantics, Disconnected channel
  path, or the 5s busy-wait on timeout.

 {"id":"ft-akx00.8","title":"Define shared verification, E2E, and artifact contract for placeholder-remediation work","description":"Define the shared verification contract for the entire `ft-akx00` program so implementers do not improvise test coverage, logging shape, artifact capture, or execution evidence on a bead-by-bead basis.

This bead exists because placeholder-remediation is high risk: many changes touch trust-sensitive control paths, platform backends, or user-facing UX surfaces. We want one reusable contract for proof, not 24 inconsistent interpretations of “tested”.","design":"1. Define the minimum unit-test standard for this program: happy path, edge/boundary behavior, explicit failure/error/cancellation modes, and repeat-run stability/idempotence where relevant.
2. Define the E2E/integration contract: each concrete bead must land either a dedicated script or an explicitly named extension to an existing shared suite.
3. Define the structured logging schema for verification runs. Minimum fields: `bead_id`, `scenario_id`, `surface`, `step`, `status`, `duration_ms`, `correlation_id`, `backend`, `platform`, `artifact_dir`, and redaction markers.
4. Define the artifact manifest for every verification run. Minimum files: `commands.txt`, `env.txt`, `structured.log`, `stdout.txt`, `stderr.txt`, `summary.json`, plus surface-specific artifacts like screenshots, pixel diffs, JSON payload snapshots, or remote traces.
5. Define naming conventions for new E2E scripts and artifact directories so failures are easy to browse.
6. Require heavy compile/test/clippy/benchmark workloads to run via `rch exec -- ...`, with worker identity and command transcript captured in evidence.","acceptance_criteria":"- The shared verification contract is explicit enough that every concrete `ft-akx00.*.*` task can reference it without ambiguity.
- Proposed E2E suite names and artifact directory conventions exist for every track in the program.
- The contract includes structured logging, artifact capture, redaction, and reproduction-command requirements.
- The contract is reflected back into the existing `ft-akx00` epics/tasks via updated acceptance criteria, notes, or comments.","notes":"Reopened: stalled 13-22 days with no recent activity. Prior claimant likely inactive.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyTiger","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:25.228541Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:34:16.480153Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:34:16.479984Z","close_reason":"Completed shared verification contract","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","stub-audit","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-akx00.8","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:25.228541Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2773,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.8","author":"jemanuel","text":"Suggested suite map for future implementers:
- machine-control/client/sdk -> `scripts/e2e_machine_control_*.sh`
- operator/startup/backup -> `scripts/e2e_ops_*.sh`
- connector/search -> `scripts/e2e_search_*.sh` and `scripts/e2e_connector_*.sh`
- core/FTUI -> `scripts/e2e_core_*.sh` and TUI snapshot fixtures
- rendering -> `scripts/e2e_render_*.sh` plus screenshot/pixel-diff artifacts
- mux/SSH -> `scripts/e2e_mux_*.sh` and `scripts/e2e_ssh_*.sh`

Completion evidence for any future bead under this epic should be considered incomplete unless it records exact commands, artifact paths, and enough logs to diagnose failure without rerunning locally.","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:25Z"},{"id":2821,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.8","author":"IndigoFox","text":"Progress update: tightened the shared placeholder-remediation verification contract in `tests/e2e/lib_rch_guards.sh` and the owned ft-akx00 harnesses for 6.2 / 6.4. The concrete changes are: 1) explicit probe/smoke log path helpers, 2) extractor helpers for selected worker / probe worker inventory / sync duration / remote duration / exit code, 3) a sentinel smoke log when `RCH_SKIP_SMOKE_PREFLIGHT=1`, and 4) owned harness summaries now record `rch_probe`, `rch_smoke`, and per-step `*.rch_meta.json` sidecars. Verified this contract concretely with `tests/e2e/test_ft_akx00_6_2_backend_resize_framebuffer.sh`, artifact root `artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.6.2/backend_resize_framebuffer/20260403T182413Z`.","created_at":"2026-04-03T18:28:32Z"},{"id":2822,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.8","author":"IndigoFox","text":"Follow-up validation: the tightened contract was also exercised via `tests/e2e/test_ft_akx00_6_4_terminfo_image_rendering.sh`, artifact root `artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.6.4/terminfo_image_rendering/20260403T182902Z`. That run produced a complete `summary.json` plus `rch_probe` / `rch_smoke` / per-step `*.rch_meta.json` sidecars even on failure. Results were 3 pass / 1 fail; the failing leg was remote clippy, and the failure was unrelated repo lint debt in `frankenterm/dynamic/derive/src/attr.rs` (`clippy::collapsible_match`), not a break in the contract plumbing itself. This gives the ft-akx00 contract both an honest failure-path example and a passing-step example with captured worker identity and durations.","created_at":"2026-04-03T18:36:45Z"},{"id":3524,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.8","author":"SunnyTiger","text":"SunnyTiger 2026-04-19: standardized the shared placeholder-remediation verification contract in repo docs. Canonical references are now `docs/test-logging-contract.md` (new `ft-akx00` profile with required JSONL fields, artifact set, and reproduction-command rules) and `docs/e2e-harness-spec.md` (new bead-scoped verification profile, required artifact root, suite map for tracks 2-7, and redaction/evidence requirements). Also extended `tests/e2e/lib_rch_guards.sh` metadata sidecars to record `repo_root`, `smoke_target_dir`, timeout settings, and whether smoke preflight was explicitly requested to be skipped, so the docs match auditable on-disk evidence instead of chat-only expectations.","created_at":"2026-04-19T14:32:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-arhlm","title":"[MEDIUM] MCP missing-golden panic still points operators at the wrong regeneration command","description":"Commit f6e7cc15 corrected the drift-path regeneration command, but the missing-golden panic still tells operators to run `UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 cargo test --test mcp_manifest_golden` (`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_manifest_golden.rs:200-205`). In this workspace, default features enable a much wider surface than the minimal review lane, so regenerating via that command can produce a different manifest than the intended `--no-default-features --features mcp,asupersync-runtime` path. This leaves the test with two conflicting operator instructions and can regenerate the wrong golden when the fixture is absent. Expected fix: make the missing-file panic use the same explicit feature-set command as the drift path and top-of-file documentation.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:58:05.098815Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:01:26.813721Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-b5rbd","title":"[HIGH] GUI resize increment saturation can overflow backend min-size arithmetic","description":"Commit 193a9ebb changes `ResizeIncrementCalculator` to saturate `base_width`/`base_height` at the u16 ceiling (`crates/frankenterm-gui/src/resize_increment_calculator.rs:15-35`). Multiple window backends then immediately compute `base + increment` in u16 without guarding overflow (`frankenterm/window/src/os/macos/window.rs:1410-1412`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/x11/window.rs:1892-1893`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/wayland/window.rs:915-916`). With large padding/border/tab-bar geometry, the new saturation can produce `base_width == u16::MAX`, so `base_width + x` overflows before min-size clamping. This turns an intended overflow hardening change into a runtime panic/wraparound path on oversized layouts. Expected fix: clamp stored base dimensions to `u16::MAX - increment` (or widen backend arithmetic) and add a regression test that asserts `base + increment` stays representable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:58:04.554054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:42:08.945504Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:42:08.945232Z","close_reason":"dup of ft-htf0i","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-b677n","title":"[MEDIUM] wgpu readback poll loop has no test coverage for timeout, disconnect, or error-propagation paths","description":"Commit 6f41130c (br-43bbl fix wgpu readback polling) rewrites the
readback wait in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:168-193
from:

\`\`\`rust
self.device.poll(PollType::Wait { submission_index: None, timeout: Some(WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT) })?;
receiver.recv_timeout(WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT)?...
\`\`\`

to a bounded poll loop:

\`\`\`rust
let deadline = Instant::now() + WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT;
loop {
    self.device.poll(wgpu::PollType::Poll)?;
    match receiver.recv_timeout(Duration::from_millis(10)) {
        Ok(result) => { result?; break; }
        Err(RecvTimeoutError::Timeout) if Instant::now() < deadline => continue,
        Err(RecvTimeoutError::Timeout) => return Err(...),
        Err(RecvTimeoutError::Disconnected) => return Err(...),
    }
}
\`\`\`

Concerns with the new implementation:

1. \`PollType::Poll\` is non-blocking by contract in wgpu 25. The loop
   relies on \`Poll\` actually driving the \`map_async\` callback forward.
   If a future wgpu point release changes \`Poll\` to be strictly
   \"dispatch-only\" (no callback invocation), the loop would always
   hit the 5s timeout. There is no test pinning this behavior.

2. The \`Disconnected\` branch is reachable only if the \`map_async\`
   closure drops without sending — that happens e.g., if wgpu drops
   the submission without invoking the callback (device lost, internal
   error). No test exercises this path.

3. \`Instant::now() + WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT\` panics on overflow
   (impossible in practice for 5s, but worth noting as a latent
   concern). Prefer \`checked_add\`.

4. Busy-wait cost: on the timeout path, the loop spins ~500 iterations
   (5000ms / 10ms) calling \`device.poll\` each time. The prior
   \`PollType::Wait\` blocked in the driver. This is a CPU-efficiency
   regression on cancellation/device-loss paths, not a correctness
   regression.

Impact: readback behavior on wgpu failure paths (device lost, long
delay, map_async disconnect) is silently unverified. A correctness
regression in \`PollType::Poll\` semantics between wgpu versions would
silently break screenshots and readback-based features.

Fix direction:
- Add a unit-level test with a mock/stub Device that controls
  \`poll()\` return timing and can force a Disconnected on the mpsc
  channel. Assert each of the four loop outcomes.
- Consider moving the readback loop into a small helper function
  that takes a \`Fn()\` poll closure + channel receiver, to make it
  unit-testable without a real GPU.
- If unit isolation is impractical, at minimum add an integration
  test that triggers a readback and asserts completion within a
  tight CPU budget (regression test for busy-wait blow-up).

Severity: medium — latent risk in a GPU-facing path that typically
works and has no automated coverage for failure modes.

Evidence: commit 6f41130c diff;
crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:168-193 at HEAD.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:25:33.419495Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:25:33.419495Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gpu","review","test-gap","testing","webgpu"]}
+{"id":"ft-cbeyl","title":"[MEDIUM] mcp_manifest_golden db-gated tool whitelist silently drifts when tool renamed or removed","description":"In commit 1bde42b3 (ft-nnfwn: freeze no-db mcp manifest surface),
\`mcp_manifest_matches_golden_without_db\` at
crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_manifest_golden.rs:254-316 hard-codes
three whitelists of tools/resources/templates expected to be db-gated:

\`\`\`rust
for expected_absent_tool in [
    \"wa.accounts\",
    \"wa.events\",
    \"wa.reserve\",
    \"wa.release\",
    \"wa.search\",
    \"wa.send\",
    \"wa.workflow_run\",
] {
    assert!(
        !no_db_tools.contains(expected_absent_tool),
        \"db-gated tool unexpectedly advertised without db_path: {expected_absent_tool}\"
    );
}
\`\`\`

The assertion only checks absence from \`no_db_tools\`. If a tool is
renamed (e.g., \`wa.accounts\` → \`wa.list_accounts\`) or removed entirely,
the whitelist entry silently stays true (\`!no_db_tools.contains(\"wa.accounts\")\`
is trivially true when \`wa.accounts\` no longer exists in either manifest).

The same drift pattern applies to the resource whitelist
(lines ~285, \`wa://accounts\`, \`wa://events\`, \`wa://reservations\`) and
the template whitelist (lines ~295, \`wa://accounts/{service}\` etc.).

Impact: the no-db subset guard gives false confidence. A tool could
accidentally become always-on (or be removed from db-gated registration)
without the test catching it.

Fix: for each whitelist entry, assert \`full_tools.contains(entry)\`
BEFORE checking \`!no_db_tools.contains(entry)\`. That way, if the
whitelisted tool disappears from the db-backed manifest, the test
fails and forces whoever made the change to update the whitelist
deliberately.

\`\`\`rust
for expected_absent_tool in [...] {
    assert!(
        full_tools.contains(expected_absent_tool),
        \"whitelist drift: {expected_absent_tool} no longer exists in db-backed manifest — update the test\"
    );
    assert!(
        !no_db_tools.contains(expected_absent_tool),
        \"db-gated tool unexpectedly advertised without db_path: {expected_absent_tool}\"
    );
}
\`\`\`

Severity rationale: medium — test-quality issue that masks regressions
in MCP tool surface categorization. Not a correctness bug in production
code.

Evidence: commit 1bde42b3 diff lines 262-316 of the new
\`mcp_manifest_matches_golden_without_db\` test body.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:24:49.960054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:24:49.960054Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","review","test-gap","testing"]}
 {"id":"ft-d0ez0","title":"[EPIC] End-to-end swarm integration proof: demonstrate full observe-detect-react loop with live agents","description":"# Goal
Prove that FrankenTerm actually delivers on its core value proposition end-to-end: observing live AI agent panes, detecting state transitions, triggering automated workflows, and exposing actionable data through Robot Mode. Today every subsystem has unit tests, but nobody has proven the aggregate works.

# Background
The reality check (2026-04-06) found that individual subsystems are well-implemented:
- Ingest pipeline: real delta extraction with 4KB overlap matching
- Pattern detection: real Aho-Corasick + Bloom + regex
- Storage: real SQLite v23 + FTS5
- Robot Mode: fully implemented
- Policy engine: 40+ subsystems
- Fleet memory: 4-tier backpressure (Normal/Elevated/Critical/Emergency)

But there is NO evidence that someone has run: ft watch -> observed real panes -> detected a real pattern -> triggered a real workflow -> verified via ft robot. The individual subsystems are proven; the integration is not.

# Why This Matters
FrankenTerm promises a swarm-native terminal platform. Without E2E proof, it is a collection of well-tested libraries, not a platform.

# Architectural Intent
1. Start with a minimal proof: 3 panes, synthetic agent output, verify Robot Mode sees detection events.
2. Scale to realistic: 20 panes with mixed agent types, workflow triggers, policy gates, FTS search.
3. Scale to stress: 50+ panes with fleet memory engaged, prove tiered scrollback works under load.
4. Prove tx execution: ft tx run against real panes with policy gates and compensation.
5. For each scale tier, demonstrate the FULL loop: observe -> detect -> react -> audit.

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Minimal E2E (ft-d0ez0.1): ft watch + 3 panes + pattern detection + Robot Mode queries
2. 20-pane integration (ft-d0ez0.2): multi-agent detection, workflow, policy, FTS search
3. 50-pane stress (ft-d0ez0.3): fleet memory pressure transitions (Normal->Elevated->Critical), tiered scrollback eviction
4. Tx E2E (ft-d0ez0.4): ft tx run with real pane operations, compensation, audit trail
5. All tests produce structured JSON artifacts for reproducibility.
6. Tests run in CI or have clear manual reproduction instructions.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:35.214581Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:06:45.893656Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:06:45.893210Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","goal-line","integration","proof","swarm"]}
 {"id":"ft-d0ez0.1","title":"Minimal E2E proof: ft watch + 3 mock panes + pattern detection + Robot Mode verification","description":"# Goal
Create the smallest possible E2E test proving the full observation-detection-query loop works against real (or simulated) panes.

# Background
Every subsystem has strong unit tests (46,633 total), but nobody has proven the aggregate works: ft watch observing panes -> pattern engine detecting state transitions -> Robot Mode returning detection events. This is the foundational proof.

# Design
## Test script: tests/e2e/e2e_minimal_loop.py

### Pane output (use ACTUAL corpus fixture text, not paraphrases)
- Pane 0: codex.usage.reached — echo the text from corpus/codex/usage_reached.txt:
  "You've hit your usage limit. Please try again at 2026-01-20 12:34 UTC."
- Pane 1: claude_code pattern — echo the text from corpus/claude_code/usage_reached.txt:
  "rate limit reached; try again in 15 minutes"
- Pane 2: normal text with no pattern matches (e.g. "hello world")

### Steps
1. Create temp workspace dir with minimal ft.toml:
   [general]
   log_level = "debug"
   [ingest]
   poll_interval_ms = 200
   [patterns]
   packs = ["core"]
   [storage]
   db_path = "<tempdir>/ft.db"
2. Build ft binary (or use prebuilt from PATH)
3. Start ft watch --foreground --workspace <tempdir> as subprocess
4. Create 3 panes (headless mux server or WezTerm CLI)
5. Write corpus text to each pane
6. Wait for 2x poll_interval (400ms + 1s buffer = ~1.5s)
7. Run assertions via ft robot:
   a. ft robot state → assert ok:true, >= 3 panes
   b. ft robot events --limit 20 → assert >= 1 event with rule_id containing "usage" for pane 0 or 1
   c. ft robot search "usage limit" → assert >= 1 search result
   d. ft robot get-text <pane_0_id> --tail 5 → assert contains "hit your usage limit"
8. ft stop or SIGTERM the watcher
9. Cleanup tempdir

### Fallback for no-backend environments
- If neither WezTerm CLI nor headless mux server available: skip with exit code 77 (TAP skip) and message
- CI should ensure headless mux server is available

## Logging
- JSON report: tests/e2e/output/e2e_minimal_loop_<timestamp>.json
- Per-assertion: { check: "robot_state", expected: "ok=true, panes>=3", actual: <raw>, pass: bool, elapsed_ms: N }
- On failure: dump ft watch stderr, ft.db stat, all robot responses
- Python logging with JSON formatter to both file and stderr

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Script runs from clean state (no pre-existing ft.db).
2. All 4 Robot Mode assertions pass.
3. Completes in under 30 seconds.
4. Idempotent (cleanup on exit via atexit or try/finally).
5. JSON report produced.
6. On failure, output sufficient to debug without re-running.

# Implementation Notes
- Pattern rules codex.usage.reached and claude_code.usage.reached are in the built-in "core" pack.
- The headless mux server binary is crates/frankenterm-mux-server — check if it provides a PTY creation API or needs wezterm cli compatibility.
- Existing tests/e2e/ directory has Python scripts — follow existing patterns (check for test runner conventions).","notes":"Implemented tests/e2e/e2e_minimal_loop.py and verified the structured skip path via tests/e2e/output/e2e_minimal_loop_20260407T020831Z.json. Remaining validation of the live-pane success path is blocked on this machine because no usable ft binary is available locally and wezterm is not present in PATH. The script records both preconditions explicitly and exits 77 as designed.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:51.950550Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T05:00:43.138350Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T05:00:43.138183Z","close_reason":"Completed: remote offloaded build plus passing minimal E2E proof report","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","minimal","proof"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.1","depends_on_id":"ft-d0ez0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:51.950550Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2920,"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Binary discovery: The E2E script should locate the ft binary via: 1) FT_BINARY env var (explicit path), 2) cargo build -p frankenterm --release output, 3) which ft (PATH lookup). The script should fail early with a clear error if the binary is not found rather than failing obscurely during subprocess calls.","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:05:09Z"},{"id":2921,"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.1","author":"RedGate","text":"Implemented `tests/e2e/e2e_minimal_loop.py`.

Details:
- resolves `ft` as `FT_BINARY` -> cargo build outputs -> `which ft`
- reads the real codex and claude_code usage-reached corpus fixtures
- provisions an isolated temp workspace plus minimal `ft.toml`
- starts `ft watch --foreground` against that workspace
- drives three panes through WezTerm when available
- runs the required robot assertions (`state`, `events --limit 20`, `search "usage limit"`, `get-text <pane0> --tail 5`)
- writes a structured JSON report under `tests/e2e/output/`
- exits 77 with a structured skip report when the active backend is unavailable

Current environment note: local validation here cannot reach the live-pane path because `wezterm` is not in PATH. An `rch exec -- cargo build -p frankenterm --bin ft` offloaded build is in flight to validate real binary discovery before the script is exercised further.","created_at":"2026-04-07T02:01:26Z"},{"id":2922,"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.1","author":"RedGate","text":"Latest run: `python3 tests/e2e/e2e_minimal_loop.py` produced `tests/e2e/output/e2e_minimal_loop_20260407T020831Z.json` and exited `77`.

Skip artifact details:
- binary search order exercised and recorded: `FT_BINARY` -> cargo outputs -> `PATH`
- fixture paths recorded for both codex and claude_code usage corpus inputs
- combined skip reason captured: no usable `ft` binary local to this machine, and `wezterm` absent from `PATH`

The script is in place; what remains is success-path execution on a machine with both prerequisites available.","created_at":"2026-04-07T02:09:41Z"},{"id":2931,"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.1","author":"CopperHare","text":"CopperHare completion evidence:

- Patched `tests/e2e/e2e_minimal_loop.py` so the harness honors `FT_WEZTERM_CLI` consistently for backend discovery, pane spawn, and pane teardown.
- Offloaded build succeeded with explicit remote timeout increase: `env RCH_BUILD_TIMEOUT_SEC=1800 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_copperhare_ft-d0ez0-1 cargo build -p frankenterm --bin ft` on worker `vmi1264463`.
- Ran the live success-path proof on that worker against a simulated `wezterm` CLI bridge wired through `FT_WEZTERM_CLI` and the freshly built `/tmp/rch_target_copperhare_ft-d0ez0-1/debug/ft`.
- Report artifact: `tests/e2e/output/e2e_minimal_loop_20260407T045957Z.json`
- Result: `status=passed`; all required assertions were true for `robot state`, `robot events`, `robot search`, and `robot get-text`.

Notable runtime note from the passing run: `ft` selected the CLI backend because vendored metadata is currently considered stale (`vendored commit not recorded`), but that did not block the proof.","created_at":"2026-04-07T05:00:42Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-d0ez0.2","title":"20-pane integration test: multi-agent detection, workflow trigger, policy gate, FTS search verification","description":"# Goal
Scale the E2E proof to 20 panes with mixed agent types, verifying all major subsystems integrate correctly under realistic multi-agent conditions.

# Background
The minimal E2E (ft-d0ez0.1) proves the basic loop. This test proves it works at moderate scale with mixed agent types, workflow triggers, policy enforcement, and FTS search.

# Design
## Test script: tests/e2e/e2e_20pane_integration.py

### Setup
- 20 panes via headless mux server: 7 simulated Codex, 7 Claude Code, 3 Gemini, 3 plain shell
- Each agent pane receives fixture output from crates/frankenterm-core/tests/corpus/<agent>/
- Output mix: rate limits, errors, normal output, long-running ops

### Policy configuration (ft.toml for the test)
Pane 15 is policy-denied via safety config:
  [safety]
  require_prompt_active = false
  block_alt_screen = true
  [safety.rate_limits]
  send_text = { max_per_second = 0 }  # deny all sends to trigger rate limit
  # OR use a custom policy rule that denies pane_id=15 specifically

### Verification matrix (8 checks)
1. Delta extraction: ft robot get-text --all --tail 1 returns data for all 20 panes
2. Pattern detection: ft robot events --limit 50 returns rate-limit events for 17 agent panes
3. False positive check: ft robot events filtered to panes 17,18,19 returns ZERO events
4. Workflow trigger: ft workflow status shows at least 1 completed workflow execution
5. Policy gate: ft robot send 15 'test' returns ok:false with error.code=robot.policy_denied
6. FTS search: ft robot search 'rate limit' returns results spanning multiple panes
7. Robot Mode state: ft robot state returns all 20 panes with correct metadata (domain, title)
8. Audit trail: ft audit --decision deny shows the pane 15 denial record

### Timing
- Stagger pane output over 10 seconds to test temporal ordering
- Total duration target: under 60 seconds

## Logging
- JSON report: tests/e2e/output/e2e_20pane_<timestamp>.json
- Per-check: check_name, expected, actual, pass/fail, duration_ms, raw_response (first 500 chars)
- Summary: total_checks, passed, failed, elapsed_total_ms
- On failure: dump ft doctor --json, full event list, audit trail, failing check raw response

# Acceptance Criteria
1. All 8 verification checks pass.
2. Zero false-positive detections on plain shell panes.
3. At least 1 completed workflow in execution log.
4. Audit trail includes policy denial with pane_id=15.
5. Structured JSON report produced.
6. Completes in under 60 seconds.
7. On failure, diagnostic output pinpoints exactly which check failed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:57:05.594187Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T01:32:02.956948Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T01:32:02.956583Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","integration","multi-agent"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.2","depends_on_id":"ft-d0ez0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:57:05.594187Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.2","depends_on_id":"ft-d0ez0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:57:25.496062Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-d0ez0.3","title":"50-pane stress test: fleet memory pressure transitions, tiered scrollback eviction, backpressure under load","description":"# Goal
Prove fleet memory management and tiered scrollback work under sustained 50-pane load, matching the advertised 200+ pane scale claims.

# Background
The README claims '200+ panes under 1GB' and describes a 4-tier fleet memory controller. The fleet_memory_controller.rs and scrollback_tiers.rs are implemented, but never tested at scale. This test proves they work.

# Design
## Test script: tests/e2e/e2e_50pane_stress.py
### Setup
- 50 panes via headless mux server, each generating ~1KB/sec continuous output via script
- Per-pane memory budget configured to trigger tier transitions within 5 minutes
- Total sustained throughput: ~50KB/sec

### Phase 1: Warm-up (0-60s)
- All panes generating output
- Verify: ft robot state shows 50 panes, ft doctor --json shows Normal pressure tier

### Phase 2: Pressure build (60-180s)
- Continue output generation
- Monitor: FleetPressureTier transitions via ft doctor --json (polled every 10s)
- Expected: Normal -> Elevated as scrollback accumulates

### Phase 3: Tier transitions (180-300s)
- Continue output generation with increased rate on 10 panes
- Expected: Elevated -> Critical on high-output panes
- Verify: tiered scrollback eviction events occur (warm tier activation)

### Phase 4: Detection under load (at 240s)
- Inject rate-limit pattern into pane 25
- Measure: time from injection to ft robot events showing the detection
- Expected: detection latency < 5 seconds

### Phase 5: Robot Mode under load (at 270s)
- Time ft robot state, ft robot search, ft robot get-text responses
- Expected: state < 2s, search < 5s

### Metrics collection
- Peak RSS of ft process (via ps or /proc/self/status)
- Detection latency (injection time vs event timestamp)
- Robot Mode response latency
- Pressure tier transition timestamps
- Scrollback tier eviction counts

## Logging
- JSON metrics report: tests/e2e/output/e2e_50pane_stress_<timestamp>.json
- Per-phase: phase_name, start_time, end_time, metrics_snapshot
- Timeseries: pressure_tier sampled every 10s
- On failure: full metrics dump + ft doctor --json + process info

# Acceptance Criteria
1. ft process RSS stays under 1GB with 50 panes over 5 minutes.
2. Pattern detection latency < 5 seconds under 50-pane load.
3. Robot Mode state query < 2 seconds, search < 5 seconds under load.
4. At least one FleetPressureTier transition above Normal observed.
5. At least one tiered scrollback tier transition (hot->warm) observed.
6. No crashes, panics, or data corruption during 5-minute run.
7. Machine-readable metrics report produced with all measurements.
8. On failure, report identifies which metric breached which threshold.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":360,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:57:21.555623Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:06:29.576005Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:06:29.575707Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","fleet-memory","performance","scrollback","stress"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.3","depends_on_id":"ft-d0ez0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:57:21.555623Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.3","depends_on_id":"ft-d0ez0.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:57:25.736976Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2919,"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"CI note: This 5-minute test should run in a nightly CI job (not per-push) or be triggered manually. The per-push CI can run ft-d0ez0.1 (30s) and ft-d0ez0.2 (60s). Add a .github/workflows/nightly-stress.yml job for this test.","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:05:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-d0ez0.4","title":"E2E proof for ft tx run: execute a real transactional multi-pane operation and verify audit trail","description":"# Goal
Prove that ft tx run executes real pane operations transactionally, including compensation on failure, with a complete audit trail.

# Background
The tx integration tests (ft-y9lnb.5) use MockWezterm. This E2E test uses real (or headless mux server) panes to prove the full CLI->executor->pane->audit pipeline works.

# Verified Contract Format (MissionTxContract JSON)
{
  "tx_version": 1,
  "intent": { "tx_id": "test-tx-1", "requested_by": "operator", "summary": "E2E test", "correlation_id": "e2e-001", "created_at_ms": <timestamp> },
  "plan": {
    "plan_id": "plan-1", "tx_id": "test-tx-1",
    "steps": [
      { "step_id": "step-1", "ordinal": 0, "action": { "type": "send_text", "pane_id": 0, "text": "echo step1-marker" }, "description": "Send marker to pane 0" },
      { "step_id": "step-2", "ordinal": 1, "action": { "type": "send_text", "pane_id": 1, "text": "echo step2-marker" }, "description": "Send marker to pane 1" }
    ],
    "preconditions": [],
    "compensations": [
      { "for_step_id": "step-1", "action": { "type": "send_text", "pane_id": 0, "text": "echo COMPENSATED" } }
    ]
  },
  "lifecycle_state": "pending",
  "outcome": "pending",
  "receipts": []
}

# Design
## Test script: tests/e2e/e2e_tx_run.py

### Scenario 1: Happy path (2-step transaction)
1. Start ft watch with headless mux server
2. Create 2 panes with shell sessions
3. Write mission.json contract (template above)
4. Run ft tx run --contract mission.json
5. Verify: ft robot get-text 0 contains 'step1-marker'
6. Verify: ft robot get-text 1 contains 'step2-marker'
7. Verify: ft tx show --include-contract shows committed state

### Scenario 2: Partial failure with compensation
1. Same setup, but step 2 targets nonexistent pane 99
2. Run ft tx run --contract mission_fail.json
3. Verify: pane 0 contains 'COMPENSATED' (compensation ran)
4. Verify: ft tx show shows compensation state
5. Verify: ft audit shows failure + compensation records

### Scenario 3: Policy denial
1. Configure ft.toml safety to deny SendText on pane 1
2. Run ft tx run --contract mission.json
3. Verify: prepare-phase denial with policy reason in output
4. Verify: pane 0 does NOT contain 'step1-marker' (no steps executed on denial)

## Logging
- JSON report: tests/e2e/output/e2e_tx_run_<timestamp>.json
- Per-scenario: scenario_name, contract_file, ft_tx_run_stdout, ft_tx_run_stderr, ft_tx_show_output, audit_records, pane_contents, pass/fail
- On failure: full diagnostic dump

# Acceptance Criteria
1. All 3 scenarios pass.
2. Happy path shows markers in panes AND committed state in ft tx show.
3. Compensation scenario shows compensation execution in audit trail AND pane output.
4. Policy denial shows zero pane modifications.
5. Completes in under 60 seconds.
6. Structured JSON report produced.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-04-06T21:45:58.657051Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-10T22:34:12.108953Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T22:34:12.108636Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","proof","tx"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.4","depends_on_id":"ft-d0ez0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:45:58.657051Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.4","depends_on_id":"ft-d0ez0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:46:02.740774Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.4","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:46:02.533076Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.

6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. +{"id":"ft-e8brh","title":"[MEDIUM] cancel_races_child_creation test has weak race coverage; passes trivially via propagation","description":"In commit 6a16ae0a (fix(ft-2xnop): add unix umask coverage ... +
cancellation concurrency tests), the test
\`cancel_races_child_creation_all_children_see_cancellation\` at
crates/frankenterm-core/src/cancellation.rs:2635-2676 claims to verify
the cancel+child race but does not reliably exercise the race window.

Structure:
\`\`\`rust
for _ in 0..20 {
    let parent = CancellationToken::new(...);
    let n_creators = 8;
    let barrier = Arc::new(Barrier::new(n_creators + 1));

    let handles: Vec<_> = (0..n_creators).map(|i| {
        let parent = parent.clone();
        let barrier = Arc::clone(&barrier);
        thread::spawn(move || {
            barrier.wait();
            parent.child(ScopeId(format!(\"c_{i}\")))
        })
    }).collect();

    barrier.wait();
    parent.cancel(ShutdownReason::UserRequested);
    // ... assert every child is cancelled
}
\`\`\`

Problems:

1. The barrier synchronizes the START of each thread's work but not
   the duration of \`parent.child(...)\`. On a machine with >=8 cores,
   the 8 creator threads typically finish \`parent.child(...)\` BEFORE
   the main thread reaches \`parent.cancel(...)\`. In that ordering, all
   children are pre-existing when cancel propagates — a trivial case
   covered by other tests in the file.

2. 20 sequential rounds with no scheduler perturbation (no \`yield_now\`,
   no sleep, no thread count above core count) rarely hit the interesting
   interleaving where cancel runs mid-child-creation.

3. The assertion passes even if \`parent.child()\` has a bug where it
   reads the \"parent cancelled\" flag AFTER registration but BEFORE
   propagation reaches it — because the test never creates that window.

Impact: false confidence in race-safe \`CancellationToken::child()\`
under concurrent cancel. A latent bug where a newly-created child
escapes both propagation (not-yet-registered) AND post-cancel detection
(child() reads stale flag) would not be caught.

Fix direction:
- Spawn more creators than CPU cores (e.g., 64) so some are preempted
  mid-call.
- Insert \`thread::yield_now()\` or a short sleep inside the creator
  body before calling \`parent.child()\` to widen the race window.
- Alternatively, use an inner barrier that creators wait on AFTER
  they've decided to create the child but BEFORE registration, so the
  main thread can force the interleaving.
- Consider using loom or shuttle for deterministic interleaving
  coverage instead of probabilistic races.

Severity: medium — test quality issue, not a proven correctness bug
in cancellation.rs. Filing because the test claims coverage it does
not deliver.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:25:11.821489Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:25:11.821489Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cancellation","concurrency","review","test-gap","testing"]}
 {"id":"ft-gofz5","title":"[EPIC] Release engineering: first tagged release, cargo install validation, and binary distribution pipeline","description":"# Goal
Establish a working release pipeline so that \`cargo install --git ... ft\` actually works, release binaries are available for major platforms, and there are tagged versions users can reference.

# Background
The reality check found:
- Only 1 git tag exists ("backup-before-rewrite") — no version releases
- \`cargo install\` is advertised in README Quick Install but never validated
- 6 GitHub Actions workflows exist (ci.yml, release.yml, etc.) but release.yml has never been triggered for a real release
- The project is pre-1.0 with 4,288 commits but zero releases
- Build is resource-intensive (~793k LOC core library) which may cause cargo install to fail on memory-constrained machines

# Why This Matters
A project that can't be installed by its advertised method has zero users. Even for internal/swarm use, tagged releases let agents pin to known-good versions. Release hygiene signals project maturity.

# Architectural Intent
1. Validate that \`cargo install --git ... ft\` succeeds on a clean machine (Linux x86_64, macOS arm64 at minimum).
2. Create v0.1.0 tag with honest release notes reflecting current capabilities.
3. Ensure release.yml workflow produces downloadable binaries.
4. Add a CI job that runs \`cargo install\` from git to catch install regressions.

# Acceptance Criteria
1. \`cargo install --git https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm.git ft\` succeeds on Linux x86_64 and macOS arm64.
2. A v0.1.0 tag exists with release notes that honestly describe working features, partial features, and known limitations.
3. release.yml produces downloadable binaries for at least Linux x86_64 and macOS arm64.
4. CI includes a cargo install smoke test that runs on every push to main.
5. README Quick Install section includes minimum system requirements (RAM, disk, Rust nightly version).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:35.783442Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:07:18.344985Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:07:18.344593Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","distribution","goal-line","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5","depends_on_id":"ft-d0ez0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:59:00.127254Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-gofz5.1","title":"Validate cargo install from git on clean Linux x86_64 and macOS arm64 environments","description":"# Goal
Prove that the advertised Quick Install command actually works on fresh machines.

# Design
1. On a clean Linux x86_64 environment (CI runner or fresh container):
   - Install Rust nightly (matching rust-toolchain.toml)
   - Run: cargo install --git https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm.git ft
   - Verify: ft --version produces output
   - Verify: ft doctor runs without crash
   - Record: peak RAM usage, disk usage, wall time

2. On macOS arm64 (CI runner or local):
   - Same steps as above
   - Additional: verify LLVM/clang dependency resolution (cc alias issue documented in MEMORY.md)

3. If install fails, diagnose and fix:
   - Likely issues: memory exhaustion (793k LOC), missing system deps, cc alias conflict
   - Fix in Cargo.toml, build.rs, or CI configuration
   - Document minimum requirements

# Acceptance Criteria
1. cargo install succeeds on both platforms with exit code 0.
2. ft --version and ft doctor both work post-install.
3. Peak RAM and disk requirements documented.
4. If build requires CC/CXX overrides, document in README.","notes":"2026-04-07 CyanIvy: install/docs command corrected; targeted docs smoke test passes via rch; clean-host harness now passes and records WezTerm bridge prerequisites honestly; remaining gap is true clean-host cargo-install-from-git validation on Linux/macOS because rch in this environment is classifying cargo install as non-offloaded. Added workflow .github/workflows/cargo-install-validation.yml for that next step.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:46.599217Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T01:59:46.274240Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T01:59:46.261176Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","install","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-gofz5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:46.599217Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-gofz5.2","title":"Create v0.1.0 tagged release with honest release notes and downloadable binaries","description":"# Goal
Cut the first real version release with honest release notes.

# Design
1. Version bump: Set version = "0.1.0" in relevant Cargo.toml files
2. Write release notes that honestly categorize:
   - Working: observation pipeline, pattern detection, Robot Mode, FTS search, policy engine, fleet memory, tiered scrollback, BOCPD, session persistence, connector SDK
   - Feature-gated: MCP, distributed mode, semantic search, web API, TUI
   - Partial: tx execution (orchestration framework complete, data plane in progress), mission dispatch
   - Known limitations: resource-intensive build, Rust nightly required, WezTerm backend dependency
3. Trigger release.yml workflow
4. Verify downloadable binaries are produced for Linux x86_64 and macOS arm64

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Git tag v0.1.0 exists with annotated release notes.
2. GitHub Release page has downloadable binaries.
3. Release notes are honest — no feature is listed as working unless it passes end-to-end.
4. CHANGELOG.md updated with v0.1.0 section.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:02.879546Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:04:07.025577Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:04:07.022546Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["release","v0-1-0"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-gofz5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:02.879546Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-gofz5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:15.706256Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
